stories.title: A Visit to the Zoo
sentences: Today is a sunny Sunday.
Zhang Peng and John are visiting the city zoo together.
They see two tall giraffes near the big tree.
The brown giraffe is 5 metres tall.
The spotted giraffe is 4.8 metres tall.
So the brown giraffe is taller than the spotted one.
Then they see two small monkeys playing.
The brown monkey is only 5 kilograms.
The yellow monkey is 8 kilograms.
The yellow monkey is heavier and stronger.
John is 1.62 metres tall now.
Zhang Peng is 1.65 metres tall.
Zhang Peng is 3 centimetres taller than John.
They have a lot of fun at the zoo.

questions.question: The brown giraffe is taller than the spotted giraffe.
questions.explanation: The brown giraffe is 5 metres and the spotted one is 4.8 metres, so 5 is more than 4.8.

questions.question: How heavy is the yellow monkey?
questions.explanation: The story states the yellow monkey is 8 kilograms.

questions.question: Zhang Peng is 3 centimetres ________ than John.
questions.explanation: Based on the story, Zhang Peng is 1.65m and John is 1.62m, making Zhang Peng taller.

stories.title: Buying New Shoes
sentences: Spring is coming and Sarah needs new shoes.
She goes to a shoe store with her mother.
Sarah tries on a pair of red shoes.
They are size 36 but they are too small.
'What size are your shoes?' the salesperson asks.
Sarah says, 'I wore size 35 last year.'
Her mother says, 'Your feet are bigger than before.'
Sarah tries size 38 shoes this time.
These shoes are much better for her feet.
Sarah's feet are longer than her mother's feet now.
She is also becoming taller than her mother.
Sarah is very happy with her new shoes.
They walk out of the store happily.

questions.question: Sarah's feet are smaller than before.
questions.explanation: The story says her feet are bigger than before.

questions.question: What size of shoes did Sarah wear last year?
questions.explanation: Sarah says, 'I wore size 35 last year.'

questions.question: Sarah's feet are ________ than her mother's feet now.
questions.explanation: The text states: Sarah's feet are longer than her mother's feet now.

stories.title: Wu Binbin's Busy Saturday
sentences: Last Saturday, Wu Binbin stayed at home because it was rainy all day.
First, he cleaned his room and made his bed carefully.
He washed his clothes and hung them up to dry.
In the afternoon, he wanted to do something fun.
He watched TV and saw a very interesting show about robots.
Then, he read a new storybook in his study.
His mother asked, 'Did you do your homework, Binbin?'
He answered, 'Yes, I did my homework and I also cleaned the house.'
His mother was very happy and smiled at him.
They cooked a delicious dinner together in the kitchen.
Wu Binbin felt tired but he was very proud of himself.
It was a busy and wonderful Saturday for him.

questions.question: Wu Binbin went outside to play with his friends on Saturday.
questions.explanation: The story says he stayed at home because it was rainy.

questions.question: What did Wu Binbin do in the afternoon?
options: He watched TV and read a storybook.
questions.answer: He watched TV and read a storybook.
questions.explanation: The text mentions he watched TV and then read a storybook in the afternoon.

questions.question: Wu Binbin felt _____ but happy at the end of the day.
questions.explanation: The story mentions 'Wu Binbin felt tired but he was very proud'.

stories.title: A Great Weekend Trip
sentences: Last weekend, Sarah went to a park with her grandfather.
The weather was sunny and the air was very fresh.
They walked for a long time and saw many beautiful flowers.
Sarah drank some tea and talked with her grandfather.
He told her many stories about his own primary school days.
Sarah asked, 'Did you have a computer at that time?'
Her grandfather laughed and said, 'No, we didn't have computers.'
In the evening, Sarah watched children's shows on the internet.
Then, she played football with her dog in the garden.
She had a great time with her family last weekend.
She wrote a letter to her friend about her trip.
She was very excited to share her stories.

questions.question: Sarah's grandfather had a computer when he was young.
questions.explanation: Grandfather said, 'No, we didn't have computers.'

questions.question: Who did Sarah go to the park with?
questions.explanation: The first sentence says she went with her grandfather.

questions.question: Sarah played _____ with her dog in the garden.
questions.explanation: The text says 'she played football with her dog'.

stories.title: A Snowy Trip to Harbin
sentences: Last winter holiday, Sarah went to Harbin with her parents.
They went there by plane because it was very far.
The weather was very cold and it snowed a lot.
Sarah rode a horse on the ice for the first time.
She also took many photos of the beautiful ice lanterns.
In the afternoon, they ate delicious fish and hot soup.
Suddenly, Sarah fell off the sled and hurt her foot.
It didn't hurt much, but she stayed in the hotel to rest.
She saw many birds through the big window.
She also bought some gifts for her friends in Beijing.
I had a great time despite my foot, Sarah wrote in her diary.
She wants to go there again next year.

questions.question: Sarah went to Harbin by train with her friends.
questions.explanation: The story says she went there by plane with her parents.

questions.question: What did Sarah do on the ice?
questions.explanation: The text states 'Sarah rode a horse on the ice for the first time.'

questions.question: Sarah stayed in the hotel because she hurt her ________.
questions.explanation: The story mentions she 'hurt her foot' after falling off the sled.

stories.title: Wu Binbin's Busy Weekend
sentences: Wu Binbin had a very busy weekend last week.
On Saturday morning, he went to a forest park by bike.
He went with his cousin, Jack.
They saw many green trees and colorful flowers there.
They went camping and stayed there for one night.
On Sunday, they went fishing near a small lake.
Jack caught a big fish, but Wu Binbin caught nothing.
Then they licked some cold ice cream because it was hot.
In the evening, they went back home by bus.
Wu Binbin was very tired but very happy.
He did his homework after dinner.
He told his teacher about the trip on Monday.

questions.question: Who did Wu Binbin go to the park with?
questions.explanation: The story says 'He went with his cousin, Jack.'

questions.question: How did they go to the forest park?
questions.explanation: The second sentence says he 'went to a forest park by bike.'

questions.question: Wu Binbin and Jack stayed in the forest park for one night.
questions.explanation: The text says 'They went camping and stayed there for one night.'

stories.title: Grandpa's Small Village
sentences: Twenty years ago, my grandpa lived in a very small village.
There were no tall buildings and there was no internet then.
People used to walk or ride bikes because there were no cars.
But everything is different now in the village.
There is a big library and a modern gym near my grandpa's house.
The library is the biggest building in the village.
Before, Grandpa didn't like sports because he was always busy.
Now, he loves playing basketball with his friends every weekend.
He is much healthier than before.
I went to the village last summer holiday and I liked it.
It was the most beautiful place I have ever seen.
Grandpa says the village will be even better in the future.

questions.question: There was a big library in the village twenty years ago.
questions.explanation: The story says there were no tall buildings then and the library is there 'now'.

questions.question: What does Grandpa do every weekend now?
options: He rides a bike to the city.
He uses the internet at home.
He works in the tall building.
questions.explanation: The text states: 'Now, he loves playing basketball with his friends every weekend.'

questions.question: What is the biggest building in the village now?
questions.explanation: The sentence states 'The library is the biggest building in the village.'

stories.title: Wu Binbin's Change
sentences: Wu Binbin was a quiet boy three years ago.
He was short and he could not run fast.
Before, he didn't like PE class at all.
He thought he was the slowest runner in his class.
Now, Wu Binbin is very active and tall.
He practiced running every morning last year.
Now he loves sports and he is the fastest runner in school.
There was no gym in his school before.
But now there is a large gym with many stars on the wall.
Wu Binbin also liked to stay at home before.
Now he likes to go cycling with his active friends.
He is very happy about his new life.

questions.question: Wu Binbin was very active three years ago.
questions.explanation: The story says Wu Binbin was a 'quiet boy' three years ago, not active.

questions.question: Which word describes Wu Binbin's running speed now?
questions.explanation: The text says 'Now he loves sports and he is the fastest runner in school.'

questions.question: What new facility is in the school now that wasn't there before?
questions.explanation: The story mentions 'There was no gym... But now there is a large gym'.

stories.title: Wu Binbin's Busy Sunday
sentences: Yesterday was Sunday and Wu Binbin had a very busy day.
In the morning, he cleaned his room and washed his clothes.
Then, he went to a supermarket to buy some food.
He bought an apple, a watermelon and some beef for lunch.
After that, he visited his grandparents and played football with his cousin.
Finally, he did his homework and went to bed at nine o'clock.
Today, he is talking about his weekend with his friends at school.
He feels a little tired but he is very happy.
He likes to help his parents with the housework on weekends.
His mother gave him a small gift for being so helpful.
He thinks it is important to review what he learned every day.

questions.question: Wu Binbin went to the supermarket before he visited his grandparents.
questions.explanation: According to the story, he went to the supermarket 'Then' and visited grandparents 'After that'.

questions.question: What did Wu Binbin buy at the supermarket?
options: an apple, a watermelon and some beef
questions.answer: an apple, a watermelon and some beef
questions.explanation: The text states: 'He bought an apple, a watermelon and some beef for lunch.'

questions.question: Wu Binbin went to bed at ______ o'clock.
questions.explanation: The sixth sentence mentions he went to bed at nine o'clock.

stories.title: A Great Dream for the Summer
sentences: Summer holiday is coming and Sarah has a wonderful plan.
She is going to take a trip to Beijing with her parents.
They will visit the Great Wall and take many beautiful pictures.
Sarah wants to be a reporter in the future.
She is going to write a story about her trip for a magazine.
In the morning, she will walk in the ancient parks.
Then, she will eat some traditional Chinese food in fish restaurants.
After that, she will go to an art museum to see paintings.
Finally, she will buy some postcards for her best friends.
She believes her summer holiday will be colorful and interesting.
Are you going to have a trip this summer too?

questions.question: Sarah wants to be a pilot when she grows up.
questions.explanation: The story says 'Sarah wants to be a reporter in the future.'

questions.question: What will Sarah do finally in her plan?
options: She will visit the Great Wall.
She will go to an art museum.
questions.explanation: The text says: 'Finally, she will buy some postcards for her best friends.'

questions.question: Sarah is going to take a trip to ______ with her parents.
questions.explanation: The second sentence mentions she is going to take a trip to Beijing.
